steps.text: Submit written justification requesting an exception from the external peer review policy.
Review and grant approval for the exception in consultation with the Office of Financial Resources (OFR).

preconditions.text: A justified emergency situation exists preventing standard external peer review by a Federal Advisory Committee.

constraints.text: All extramural research applications submitted to CDC are required to go through external peer review by a Federal Advisory Committee, except in justified emergency situations.

Scientific programs (including research and non-research, and intramural and extramural activities) conducted or funded by CDC are subject to external peer review for scientific and technical quality.
Core service activities, such as animal laboratory facilities or clinical pathology laboratories, may be subject to accreditation or audit review and also require peer review.

steps.text: Conduct first-level review of extramural research applications using a panel of experts to evaluate scientific and technical merit.
Conduct second-level review of extramural research applications using a separate senior advisory panel to evaluate preliminary recommendations.
Conduct peer review of intramural research and scientific programs.

preconditions.text: If requested by the CIO, external peer reviewers may also address mission relevance and impact of scientific programs.

constraints.text: Peer review of intramural research and scientific programs must address program quality, approach, direction, capability, and integrity.

If reviewers are recruited on an ad hoc basis, outside of a Federal Advisory Committee Act (FACA) committee, they must provide individual and independent comments of findings, and consensus decisions must be avoided.

steps.text: Conduct technical evaluation of contract proposals using a technical evaluation panel (TEP) of experts or chartered FACA committees.
Conduct a second-step review with the contracting officer, project officer, and TEP (if needed) to determine the competitive range and negotiate best and final offers.
Award the contract through the OFR contracting officer in consultation with the CIO director or designee.
Review and select task orders using existing OFR guidance and procedures.
Implement the policy and perform annual reporting (CIO Directors).

constraints.text: FACA committee members must provide individual and independent comments of findings.
Consensus decisions must be avoided by FACA committees.

steps.text: Implement the peer review policy within the CIO.
Report planned and completed peer review activities annually to the OS.
Ensure BSCs are available to support peer review for any Center or Division located within the CIO.
Ensure reviews are conducted by experts external to CDC, not affiliated with the program and without conflict of interest.

preconditions.text: Coordinate with CDC's Federal Advisory Committee Management Officer.

constraints.text: Reviewers must be external to CDC, not affiliated with the program, and without conflict of interest.
